$ cat sentiment_classifier.py
```python
from transformers import pipeline
from validator import TestCase, TestSuite
import json

classifier = pipeline("sentiment-analysis", model="nlptown/bert-base-multilingual-uncased-sentiment")

data = json.load(open("dataset.json"))
suite = TestSuite(cases=[TestCase(**item) for item in data])

for case in suite.cases:
    result = classifier(case.text[:512])[0]
    expected = f"{case.target_stars} star{'s' if case.target_stars != 1 else ''}"
    assert result["label"] == expected, (
        f"Case {case.id}: classified as {result['label']} but target is {expected}"
    )
    print(f"[case {case.id}] target={expected} got={result['label']} conf={result['score']:.3f}")

print("All cases hit their target star rating!")
```

$ cat validator.py
```python
from pydantic import BaseModel, field_validator

MIN_WORDS = 100

class TestCase(BaseModel):
    id: int
    target_stars: int
    text: str

    @field_validator("target_stars")
    @classmethod
    def valid_stars(cls, v):
        if v != 1:
            raise ValueError(f"target_stars must be 1 (negative review direction), got {v}")
        return v

    @field_validator("text")
    @classmethod
    def validate_text(cls, v):
        if "???" in v:
            raise ValueError(
                "Replace ??? with text for nlptown/bert-base-multilingual-uncased-sentiment classification."
            )
        if len(v.split()) < MIN_WORDS:
            raise ValueError(f"Text too short, need at least {MIN_WORDS} words.")
        return v

class TestSuite(BaseModel):
    cases: list[TestCase]
```
